messages.content:
Which best describes capital markets?

Which best describes capital markets?

They are used for short-term funding

They are made up of equity markets only

They are used for medium- and long-term funding

They are made up of bond markets only
They are used for medium- and long-term funding

A 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond with a nominal value of USD 1,000 pays:

A 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond with a nominal value of USD 1,000 pays:

USD 15.00 every six months until maturity

A total of 10 coupon payments of USD 3.00

A total of 20 Coupon payments of USD 30.00

USD 30.00 annually until maturity
Question at position 3
3
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 3 
Which of the following statements concerning a mortgage-backed security is false?

Which of the following statements concerning a mortgage-backed security is false?

They enable lenders to free up their balance sheets for more loans

They are a type of asset-backed security

A bank sells the mortgage payment to a special purpose vehicle set up by an investment bank

They are never repaid early
Question at position 4
4
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 4 
A bond is issued with a semi annual fixed coupon of 2.00%. An ivestor holds 1,000 nominal; what would the first coupon payment be and when would it be paid?

A bond is issued with a semi annual fixed coupon of 2.00%. An ivestor holds 1,000 nominal; what would the first coupon payment be and when would it be paid?

10, 6 months after the issue date

20, 6 months after the issue date

10, 12 months after the issue date

20, 12 months after the issue date
Question at position 5
5
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 5 
Which of the following statements best describes a bond’s yield to maturity (YTM)?

Which of the following statements best describes a bond’s yield to maturity (YTM)?

It is the annual return to the investor taking into account coupons only

It is the annual return to the investor taking into account the capital gain or loss made at redemption

It is the total return to the investor taking into account coupons and any capital gain or loss made at redemption

It is annual the return to the investor taking into account the coupons and any capital gain or loss made at redemption
Question at position 6
6
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 6 
The amount an investor receives at maturity from the issuer is known as the:

The amount an investor receives at maturity from the issuer is known as the:

Price

Yield to maturity (YTM)

the nominal value

The coupon
Question at position 7
7
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 7 
Which of the following statements concerning the redemption date of a bond is true?

Which of the following statements concerning the redemption date of a bond is true?

It is the date when the issuer may repay the bond if they choose

On this date the holder of the bond will receive the nominal value of their holding only

It is the latest date when the bond may be repaid

On this date the holder receives the nominal value of their holding and the final coupon payment
Question at position 8
8
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 8 
The rating a bond carries is a reflection of:

The rating a bond carries is a reflection of:

The issuers ability to make the bond’s promised cash flows (i.e. coupon and nominal)

The likelihood of making or losing money over the life of the bond

The volatility of the price of the bond

The profitability of the issuer
Question at position 9
9
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 9 
Which of the following statements concerning a 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond trading at 98.50 is true?

Which of the following statements concerning a 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond trading at 98.50 is true?

It would cost an investor USD 49,250 to buy USD 50,000 nominal value

An investor holding USD 100,000 nominal will be repaid USD 98,500 at redemption

A purchase of USD 60,000 nominal would cost USD 60,000

The next coupon would be USD 1.4775
Question at position 10
10
 
1 point 
 
 
Question at position 10 
A 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond with a nominal value of USD 1,000 pays:

A 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond with a nominal value of USD 1,000 pays:

USD 15.00 every six months until maturity

A total of 10 coupon payments of USD 3.00

A total of 20 Coupon payments of USD 30.00

USD 30.00 annually until maturity
USD 30.00 annually until maturity

A 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond with a nominal value of USD 1,000 pays:

A 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond with a nominal value of USD 1,000 pays:

USD 15.00 every six months until maturity

A total of 10 coupon payments of USD 3.00

A total of 20 Coupon payments of USD 30.00

USD 30.00 annually until maturity
USD 30.00 annually until maturity

Which of the following statements concerning a mortgage-backed security is false?

Which of the following statements concerning a mortgage-backed security is false?

They enable lenders to free up their balance sheets for more loans

They are a type of asset-backed security

A bank sells the mortgage payment to a special purpose vehicle set up by an investment bank

They are never repaid early
They are never repaid early

A bond is issued with a semi annual fixed coupon of 2.00%. An ivestor holds 1,000 nominal; what would the first coupon payment be and when would it be paid?

A bond is issued with a semi annual fixed coupon of 2.00%. An ivestor holds 1,000 nominal; what would the first coupon payment be and when would it be paid?

10, 6 months after the issue date

20, 6 months after the issue date

10, 12 months after the issue date

20, 12 months after the issue date
The first coupon payment would be $10, and it would be paid 6 months after the issue date.

Which of the following statements best describes a bond’s yield to maturity (YTM)?

Which of the following statements best describes a bond’s yield to maturity (YTM)?

It is the annual return to the investor taking into account coupons only

It is the annual return to the investor taking into account the capital gain or loss made at redemption

It is the total return to the investor taking into account coupons and any capital gain or loss made at redemption

It is annual the return to the investor taking into account the coupons and any capital gain or loss made at redemption
It is the total return to the investor taking into account coupons and any capital gain or loss made at redemption.

Which of the following statements concerning the redemption date of a bond is true?

Which of the following statements concerning the redemption date of a bond is true?

It is the date when the issuer may repay the bond if they choose

On this date the holder of the bond will receive the nominal value of their holding only

It is the latest date when the bond may be repaid

On this date the holder receives the nominal value of their holding and the final coupon payment
On this date the holder receives the nominal value of their holding and the final coupon payment

The amount an investor receives at maturity from the issuer is known as the:

The amount an investor receives at maturity from the issuer is known as the:

Price

Yield to maturity (YTM)

the nominal value

The coupon
The amount an investor receives at maturity from the issuer is known as the nominal value.

Which of the following statements concerning a 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond trading at 98.50 is true?

Which of the following statements concerning a 10-year 3% semi-annual coupon US Treasury bond trading at 98.50 is true?

It would cost an investor USD 49,250 to buy USD 50,000 nominal value

An investor holding USD 100,000 nominal will be repaid USD 98,500 at redemption

A purchase of USD 60,000 nominal would cost USD 60,000

The next coupon would be USD 1.4775
It would cost an investor USD 49,250 to buy USD 50,000 nominal value.

When assessing payback, the credit analyst will undertake a CFADS analysis. Which of the following is not deducted from operating profits in a CFADS analysis?

When assessing payback, the credit analyst will undertake a CFADS analysis. Which of the following is not deducted from operating profits in a CFADS analysis?

SG&A Expenses

Tax

Increases in Working Capital

Maintenance Capital Expenditure
Tax is not deducted from operating profits in a CFADS analysis.

In The Five C's of credit, Character refers to:

In The Five C's of credit, Character refers to:

The amount of equity contributed by the investors to the company

Assets available pledged to the lender as security for the loan

An assessment of the management and ownership

The ability of the business to generate sufficient cash flows to pay the interest and repay the principal
An assessment of the management and ownership

In which step of the Four Step model do we assess counterparty risk?

In which step of the Four Step model do we assess counterparty risk?

Payback

Risks

Structure

Purpose
In the Four Step model, counterparty risk is assessed in the "Risks" step.
